steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage assumes high-sugar flowers are especially attractive, but then introduces “But”—the research found visit frequencies weren't influenced by nectar traits. Instead, physical compatibility more strongly predicted visits. This shift from nectar-driven to shape-driven is the critical signal.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: B states that the strongest floral influence (physical compatibility) may result in hummingbirds visiting low-nectar flowers over high-nectar ones—exactly what follows if shape matters more than reward. A overstates to “prefer low” nectar, C incorrectly calls nectar “secondary” when it didn't influence visits, and D invents a condition about uncertainty. That matches choice B — B is correct.
